title: Multiple Results Items to Watch Out For
description:
Multiple Results Items to Watch Out For

Multiple Results Items to Watch Out For

While multiple results are very powerful, there are some limitations with the current multiple results feature set which will be alleviated in the future but which you should be aware of at this time.

			There is currently no way to sort the multiple results except by the default sorting method. (by Area or Score) This means that you cannot depend upon the sort order of the multiple results to be based upon position. You can however write a SPEL program to determine the position of each found feature and then sort based on position from SPEL.
			There is no way to refer to the nth result of a specific object and associate that result with another object. For example, if you wanted to use the 3rd result record from a Correlation object to define a Frame, you have no way to define the frame in this way (at this time). At the time of this writing, using an object to attach to another object always uses the 1st results record.
